verdict_reasoning: C3 is a secreted plasma protein synthesized in hepatocytes and circulating at ~1.2 mg/mL. It has no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. While activated C3b can form transient covalent thioester bonds with cell surfaces via binding to antigens/immune complexes, the native C3 protein body is not stably anchored to the plasma membrane via its own mechanism—it remains in equilibrium with the soluble pool. No documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced ecto-display; no narrow germline/developmental/somatic display; not a lysosomal TM protein; no PM cycling pool independent of transient antigen attachment; no stable TM-partner tethering.
